judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have different PFS compared to those not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit and its association with progression outcomes in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer is a long-established standard oncology finding.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have different PFS compared to those not receiving treatment_palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improves progression-free survival in ER-positive/HER2-negative advanced breast cancer and is a standard guideline-supported association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have different PFS compared to those not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit and altered progression outcomes in HER2-positive breast cancer are canonical textbook and guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have different PFS compared to those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in germline BRCA-mutated HER2-negative breast cancer, including progression-free survival improvement, is an established standard association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have different PFS compared to those not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan has established progression-free survival benefit in metastatic triple-negative and selected HR-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer settings.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different PFS compared to those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1-positive metastatic triple-negative breast cancer and high-risk early triple-negative breast cancer is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients with positive er_positive have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Estrogen receptor positivity is a standard prognostic and predictive breast cancer biomarker associated with treatment response and progression outcomes.

judgments.text: Patients with positive pr_positive have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Progesterone receptor positivity is a well-established breast cancer biomarker linked to hormone receptor biology, prognosis, and endocrine responsiveness.

judgments.text: Patients with positive her2_positive have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity is a canonical breast cancer subtype-defining biomarker with established prognostic and treatment-response implications.

judgments.text: Patients with positive ki67_pct have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Ki-67 proliferation index is a widely recognized prognostic and risk-stratification marker in breast cancer, including associations with progression outcomes.

judgments.text: Patients with positive tumor_size_cm have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Tumor size is an established prognostic clinical factor for progression and survival outcomes in breast and other solid tumors.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease is a standard adverse prognostic factor and routinely associated with different PFS.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases are a well-established adverse prognostic feature associated with shorter PFS.

judgments.text: Patients with node_positive have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: Nodal positivity is a standard staging and prognostic factor in oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with ecog_ps have different PFS compared to those without.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a widely established prognostic factor for PFS and survival.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab on PFS differs by her2_positive status.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity is the canonical predictive biomarker for benefit from trastuzumab.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_palbociclib on PFS differs by her2_positive status.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib use is conventionally defined in HR-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer, making HER2 status a standard treatment-context modifier.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on PFS differs by er_positive status.
judgments.rationale: ER positivity is the established predictive biomarker for benefit from tamoxifen endocrine therapy.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on PFS differs by pr_positive status.
judgments.rationale: PR status is not an established predictive biomarker for olaparib benefit, which is conventionally linked to BRCA/HRD biology.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_palbociclib on PFS differs by her2_low status.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status as a modifier of palbociclib PFS benefit is a subgroup-specific claim beyond standard CDK4/6 treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on PFS differs by brca1_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation status as a predictor of differential benefit from olaparib on progression-free survival is an established PARP-inhibitor oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on PFS differs by brca2_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a predictor of differential benefit from olaparib on progression-free survival is an established PARP-inhibitor oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on PFS differs by brca1_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: This duplicates the established association that BRCA1 mutation status modifies olaparib benefit on progression-free survival.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_cetuximab=1 have lower pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab!=1.
judgments.rationale: An unqualified association of cetuximab treatment with worse PFS is not a standard colorectal oncology consensus claim and likely reflects subgroup or treatment-selection context.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_bevacizumab=1 have lower pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab!=1.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is an established colorectal cancer therapy, but a broad claim that treated patients have lower PFS than untreated patients is not a guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab!=1.
judgments.rationale: Improved PFS with pembrolizumab is an established finding in MSI-H/dMMR metastatic colorectal cancer and is reflected in standard treatment guidance.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_encorafenib=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib!=1.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib-based therapy improving outcomes in BRAF V600E metastatic colorectal cancer is an established guideline-supported association.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have lower pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib!=1.
judgments.rationale: A broad association of trastuzumab-tucatinib treatment with lower PFS is not an established consensus claim and would require context such as line of therapy or HER2 subgroup selection.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_regorafenib=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib!=1.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib providing PFS benefit in refractory metastatic colorectal cancer is an established treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with sex_female=1 have lower pfs_months than those with sex_female!=1.
judgments.rationale: Female sex as a predictor of lower PFS is not a standard consensus prognostic association in colorectal cancer references.

judgments.text: Patients with ecog_ps=1 have lower pfs_months than those with ecog_ps!=1.
judgments.rationale: Worse performance status such as ECOG 1 versus lower ECOG is a standard adverse prognostic factor in oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have lower pfs_months than those with stage_iv!=1.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with worse progression-free outcomes is a basic established oncology prognostic principle.

judgments.text: Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have lower pfs_months than those with right_sided_primary!=1.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary colorectal cancer is a well-established adverse prognostic and predictive clinical feature compared with left-sided disease.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have lower pfs_months than those with kras_mutation!=1.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as an adverse prognostic or predictive feature in colorectal oncology is an established association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab affecting progression-free survival in colorectal cancer, especially in molecularly selected disease, is standard clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: A stage IV-specific modification of cetuximab benefit is a subgroup interaction claim beyond the standard cetuximab/RAS paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in appropriate colorectal cancer subgroups such as MSI-H/dMMR disease is an established treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: A stage IV-specific modification of pembrolizumab effect is a conditional interaction not typically stated as consensus guidance.

judgments.text: Patients with age_years=1 have lower pfs_months than those with age_years!=1.
judgments.rationale: Age as a general prognostic factor for progression or survival in oncology is not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Patients with cea_ng_ml=1 have higher pfs_months than those with cea_ng_ml!=1.
judgments.rationale: Higher CEA being associated with higher progression-free survival conflicts with the usual adverse-prognostic CEA paradigm and is not standard consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with albumin_g_dl=1 have lower pfs_months than those with albumin_g_dl!=1.
judgments.rationale: Low or abnormal albumin as an adverse prognostic marker in cancer outcomes is well established.

judgments.text: Patients with ldh_u_l=1 have lower pfs_months than those with ldh_u_l!=1.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker for cancer progression or survival is well established.

judgments.text: Patients with weight_loss_pct_6mo=1 have higher pfs_months than those with weight_loss_pct_6mo!=1.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss being associated with higher progression-free survival is contrary to the established cachexia and poor-prognosis paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is an established colorectal cancer therapy with known effects on progression-free survival in advanced disease.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: A stage-IV-specific interaction modifying bevacizumab benefit is a subgroup-conditional claim not part of standard consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have lower pfs_months than those with stage_iv!=1.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease is a well-established adverse prognostic factor for progression-free survival.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have lower pfs_months than those with msi_high!=1.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is classically discussed as prognostic and immunotherapy-predictive, but a simple claim of lower PFS is not standard consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib has established progression-free survival activity in refractory metastatic colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is modified by age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent modification of regorafenib effect is a subgroup interaction beyond routine guideline or textbook consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have lower pfs_months than those with kras_mutation!=1.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation is a standard colorectal cancer biomarker associated with anti-EGFR resistance and adverse outcome patterns.

judgments.text: Patients with braf_v600e=1 have lower pfs_months than those with braf_v600e!=1.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is a well-established poor-prognosis biomarker in colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib-based therapy is an established targeted treatment for BRAF V600E metastatic colorectal cancer with known outcome benefit.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Stage-IV modification of encorafenib effect is a conditional treatment-effect hypothesis not established as standard consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: A broad association between pembrolizumab treatment and improved progression-free survival is an established oncology paradigm for approved immunotherapy-responsive settings.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_sotorasib=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_sotorasib=0.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant cancers is a standard targeted-therapy association rather than a novel functional-genomics hypothesis.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_olaparib=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_olaparib=0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in homologous-recombination repair deficient or BRCA-altered cancers is an established PARP-inhibitor treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_osimertinib=1 have higher pfs_months than those with treatment_osimertinib=0.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving outcomes in EGFR-mutant lung cancer is a canonical guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Patients with age_years in higher quartile have higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A claim that older age quartiles have higher progression-free survival is not a standard consensus association and would be context-dependent.

judgments.text: Patients with sex_female in higher quartile have higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A quartile-based female-sex association with higher progression-free survival is not a canonical guideline-level oncology or functional-genomics claim.

judgments.text: Patients with smoking_status in higher quartile have higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A monotonic smoking-status quartile association with improved progression-free survival is not a standard consensus finding and depends on coding and disease context.

judgments.text: Patients with ecog_ps in higher quartile have higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status usually indicates worse functional status, so a claim of higher progression-free survival is nonstandard and context-specific.

judgments.text: Patients with histology in higher quartile have higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A quartile-based histology association with higher progression-free survival is not a general established oncology paradigm without specifying lineage and coding.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent heterogeneity of pembrolizumab effect is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond the standard pembrolizumab efficacy association.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by sex_female.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of pembrolizumab progression-free survival is not a standard consensus predictive biomarker compared with established markers such as PD-L1, MSI status, and tumor mutational burden.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by smoking_status.
judgments.rationale: Smoking history as a modifier of checkpoint inhibitor benefit, particularly through its association with higher mutational burden in lung cancer, is a well-established oncology review-level concept.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months differs by age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent variation in sotorasib progression-free survival is not an established KRAS G12C treatment paradigm or guideline-level biomarker.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months differs by sex_female.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based variation in sotorasib benefit is not a standard predictive association for KRAS G12C inhibitor response.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months differs by smoking_status.
judgments.rationale: Although KRAS G12C lung cancers are enriched in smokers, smoking-status-specific differences in sotorasib treatment effect are not an established consensus therapeutic biomarker.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent differences in olaparib progression-free survival are not part of the established PARP inhibitor sensitivity paradigm centered on homologous recombination deficiency and BRCA1/2 alterations.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by sex_female.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of olaparib benefit is not a standard guideline or textbook predictive biomarker for PARP inhibitor response.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by smoking_status.
judgments.rationale: Smoking-status-specific modification of olaparib benefit is not an established PARP inhibitor response paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_osimertinib on pfs_months differs by age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent modification of osimertinib progression-free survival is not a standard predictive biomarker beyond established EGFR activating mutation status and resistance mechanisms.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_osimertinib on pfs_months differs by sex_female.
